filled.go_back_to_stem_lab_tool_list: Назад до списку інструментів STEM Lab

missing.how_sharp_or_flat_you_are_aim_for_the_: How sharp or flat you are. Aim for the green center zone (±10 cents).
missing.your_pitch_over_time_mapped_to_musical: Your pitch over time, mapped to musical notes. Green = on pitch, yellow = close, red = off.
missing.piano_roll_showing_pitch_history_green: Piano roll showing pitch history. Green line indicates on-pitch singing (within 10 cents), yellow indicates close (within 25 cents), red indicates off-pitch (beyond 25 cents).
missing.click_a_note_to_hear_the_tone_try_to_m: Click a note to hear the tone. Try to match it with your voice!
missing.a_tone_plays_match_it_with_your_voice_: A tone plays — match it with your voice! Hold the correct pitch for 1 second. Score is based on speed.
missing.sing_this_note_and_hold_it_within_10_c: Sing this note and hold it within 10 cents for 1 second
missing.this_will_guide_you_through_finding_yo: This will guide you through finding your lowest and highest comfortable notes. Make sure your microphone is on.
missing.hold_the_note_steady_for_about_1_secon: Hold the note steady for about 1 second.
missing.now_sing_your_highest_comfortable_note: Now sing your HIGHEST comfortable note
missing.estimated_from_your_lowest_note_true_v: Estimated from your lowest note — true voice type also depends on comfortable range (tessitura) and timbre, not range alone.
missing.piano_keyboard_showing_vocal_range_use: Piano keyboard showing vocal range. Use Left/Right arrows to navigate notes, Enter or Space to play.
missing.very_little_pitch_variation_good_for_c: Very little pitch variation. Good for choral blend!
missing.vibrato_rate_is_above_8_hz_try_to_rela: Vibrato rate is above 8 Hz. Try to relax and slow down.
missing.vibrato_rate_is_below_4_hz_try_to_ener: Vibrato rate is below 4 Hz. Try to energize your airflow.
missing.vibrato_width_exceeds_100_cents_try_fo: Vibrato width exceeds 100 cents. Try for a narrower oscillation.
missing.beautiful_rate_and_depth_are_in_the_id: Beautiful! Rate and depth are in the ideal range.
missing.getting_there_aim_for_5_7_hz_rate_and_: Getting there! Aim for 5-7 Hz rate and 30-80 cents depth.
missing.sustain_a_note_with_no_vibrato_practic: Sustain a note with NO vibrato. Practice controlling your voice to hold it perfectly still.
missing.sustain_a_note_and_let_your_natural_vi: Sustain a note and let your natural vibrato emerge. Don't force it — relax and let it happen.
missing.try_to_vibrate_at_exactly_6_hz_6_oscil: Try to vibrate at exactly 6 Hz (6 oscillations per second). Watch the rate readout.
missing.try_to_keep_vibrato_depth_around_50_ce: Try to keep vibrato depth around 50 cents. Not too wide, not too narrow.
missing.sustain_a_note_to_see_your_vibrato_pat: Sustain a note to see your vibrato pattern. The center line is your average pitch.
missing.vibrato_waveform_trace_showing_pitch_d: Vibrato waveform trace showing pitch deviation over time
missing.staff_notation_showing_reference_targe: Staff notation showing reference, target, and your current note
missing.explore_how_your_body_makes_sound_clic: Explore how your body makes sound. Click a region to learn about it, or turn on animations to see it in action.
missing.sagittal_cross_section_diagram_of_the_: Sagittal cross-section diagram of the human vocal tract. Use Left/Right arrows to cycle regions, Enter or Space to select.
missing.understanding_these_conditions_helps_y: Understanding these conditions helps you recognize warning signs early. If you experience persistent symptoms, see a doctor.
missing.this_information_is_educational_only_i: 👨‍⚕️ This information is educational only. It is not medical advice. If you have concerns about your voice, see an otolaryngologist (ENT doctor) or speech-language pathologist.
missing.get_personalized_vocal_health_tips_app: Get personalized vocal health tips appropriate for your grade level.
missing.breathe_deep_into_your_belly_diaphragm: Breathe deep into your belly (diaphragm), not your chest.
missing.keep_your_core_engaged_to_control_airf: Keep your core engaged to control airflow while singing.
missing.let_the_air_flow_smoothly_and_evenly_t: Let the air flow smoothly and evenly through your vocal folds.
missing.feet_shoulder_width_apart_weight_balan: Feet shoulder-width apart, weight balanced evenly
missing.shoulders_relaxed_and_rolled_back_not_: Shoulders relaxed and rolled back (not hunched)
missing.chin_level_not_tilted_up_or_down: Chin level — not tilted up or down
missing.jaw_relaxed_and_slightly_dropped_when_: Jaw relaxed and slightly dropped when singing
missing.avoid_locking_knees_tensing_neck_or_cl: Avoid: locking knees, tensing neck, or clenching jaw
missing.read_the_notes_on_the_staff_and_sing_t: Read the notes on the staff and sing them in order. Real-time pitch detection checks your accuracy.
missing.pitch_training_vocal_range_vibrato_int: Pitch training, vocal range, vibrato & interval singing
missing.sing_into_the_mic_the_lab_tracks_frequ: Sing into the mic; the lab tracks frequency in real time. Trained singers hold pitch within ±5 cents (1/20th of a semitone) — that is what perfect-pitch matching looks like.
missing.most_untrained_voices_span_1_5_2_octav: Most untrained voices span 1.5–2 octaves; trained classical singers reach 3+. Range expansion is mostly about REGISTER blending, not just stretching higher and lower.
missing.healthy_vibrato_5_7_hz_oscillation_60_: Healthy vibrato: 5–7 Hz oscillation, ~60–100 cents wide. Slower = wobble; faster = tremor. Vibrato is a function of relaxed breath support, not a separate technique.
missing.major_3rd_vs_minor_3rd_is_the_single_m: Major 3rd vs minor 3rd is the single most-confused interval pair for beginners. Mnemonics: M3 = "Oh when the saints," m3 = "Greensleeves." Builds relative pitch.
missing.lip_trills_sirens_before_any_sustained: Lip trills + sirens before any sustained singing. 5–10 minutes of low-intensity warm-up halves the risk of vocal-fold strain. Skipping warm-ups is the #1 cause of preventable vocal injury.
missing.solf_ge_do_re_mi_letter_names_for_sigh: Solfège (do-re-mi) > letter names for sight-singing because the syllables encode interval relationships, not just pitch labels. Movable do beats fixed do for tonal music.
missing.resonance_lab_discover_your_vocal_tone: Resonance Lab — discover your vocal tone
missing.two_sliders_throat_openness_and_soft_p: Two sliders — throat openness and soft palate lift — combine into four tone qualities (rich, bright, warm, nasal). No score, no reveal: experiment, log, and explain what you find.
missing.sliders_for_throat_openness_soft_palat: Sliders for throat openness, soft palate lift. Discrete 4-tone classification. No score, no reveal.
missing.hypothesis_what_anatomical_adjustments: Hypothesis: What anatomical adjustments produce rich tone?
missing.why_do_classical_singers_lift_the_pala: Why do classical singers lift the palate?
missing.how_does_throat_openness_affect_overto: How does throat openness affect overtone series?
missing.explain_how_resonance_cavity_shape_con: Explain how resonance cavity shape controls vocal tone.
missing.design_note_discrete_4_tone_marker_no_: Design note: discrete 4-tone marker; no acoustic score; no reveal — by design.
